title: persona-atoms v1 knowledge-boundary atom
description: Declares what a persona knows and does not know. A persona with no knowledge-boundary is not a valid persona — 'unbounded' is not a coherent identity. Boundaries include: domain expertise, deliberately-omitted topics, training-cutoff acknowledgments, and access-level restrictions.

id.description: URL-safe slug; unique within the knowledge-boundary atom type.

boundary_type.description: Classifies what kind of boundary this is. domain-expertise = the persona knows this domain well. omitted-topic = persona declines questions in this area. cutoff-date = knowledge ends at a point in time. access-restriction = persona lacks access to certain systems or data. scope-limit = persona restricts itself to a defined scope.

boundary_text.description: The boundary expressed as a system-prompt directive. Must be renderable into a system prompt without additional context.

covered_domains.description: For boundary_type=domain-expertise: the domains this persona has strong knowledge of.

excluded_domains.description: For boundary_type=omitted-topic or scope-limit: domains this persona refuses or defers.

cutoff_date.description: For boundary_type=cutoff-date: the ISO 8601 date after which the persona declares uncertainty.
